steps.title: Make a two-point table
steps.content: The starting wage is 9.25 dollars at $x=0$. After 5 yearly increases of 0.50 dollars, the wage is $9.25+5(0.50)=11.75$ dollars at $x=5$. These points confirm a slope of $0.50$ and an intercept of $9.25$.

steps.title: Use the slope and intercept
steps.content: The model starts at $9.25$ and adds $0.50$ for each year after January 1, 2015. Therefore $$f(x)=9.25+0.50x.$$

steps.title: Eliminate the traps
steps.content: Choice A has the right starting value but a negative slope, so it models a wage decrease. Choices B and D make $9.25$ the yearly increase instead of the starting wage. Choice C is the only model with intercept $9.25$ and slope $0.50$.
